hero.title: Legal Employment in Israel
hero.subtitle: Israel — Construction · Industrial · Service Sectors
hero.description:
Work Visa Georgia helps Georgian citizens find legal employment in Israel — from registration and documentation to employer presentation and departure.

Our team provides organised administrative support to candidates at every stage of the employment process.

about.title: About Work Visa Georgia
about.subtitle: International employment agency licensed by Georgia's Ministry of Labour
about.intro:
Work Visa Georgia (WVG) is an international employment agency licensed by Georgia's Ministry of Labour, specialising in the selection, preparation and presentation of Georgian candidates to foreign employers.

The company was founded in 2024 and its primary area of activity is the legal employment of Georgian citizens in Israel.

Work Visa Georgia's goal is to organise the international employment process to professional, transparent and legally sound standards — for both candidates and foreign partner companies.

To date, over 120 Georgian citizens have found employment in Israel with our assistance. The majority of our candidates work in the construction and industrial sectors, where professional skills, responsibility and reliability are of particular importance.
about.specializationText:
Work Visa Georgia is fully focused on the Israeli labour market.

Our accumulated practical experience, partner relationships and systematic recruitment process give us the ability to offer candidates real employment opportunities and employers pre-selected, verified staff.
about.selectionText:
Work Visa Georgia uses a multi-stage selection and verification system.

After registration, each candidate goes through an initial assessment stage during which their professional experience, contact details and suitability for the specific programme requirements are verified.

The next stage involves the preparation and checking of documentation.

If required, candidates undergo additional professional assessment, an interview or a practical skills check.

The final decision on accepting a candidate is always made by the foreign employer.

Work Visa Georgia does not make the final employment decision and does not provide employment guarantees.
about.partnersText:
Today Work Visa Georgia works with approximately 10 active partner companies in Israel.

Our partners operate in various sectors, including:
about.approachIntro: We believe that international employment should be:
about.approachText:
That is why we approach each candidate individually and support them from registration to departure.

Work Visa Georgia's work does not end after the candidate's departure. We continue communicating with employed workers and, where possible, assist them in adapting and resolving organisational matters.

whyUs.title: Why Work Visa Georgia?
items: Licensed by Georgia's Ministry of Labour
Recommended by candidates and partner companies

whyWvg.title: Why Work Visa Georgia?
whyWvg.intro:
Work Visa Georgia is an international employment agency licensed by Georgia's Ministry of Labour, specialising in organising the legal employment process in Israel.

Our main goal is to provide the candidate with a transparent, organised and professional service, and the employer with a pre-selected, verified and prepared worker.

sections.title: Officially Licensed Agency
sections.text:
Work Visa Georgia holds an official licence issued by Georgia's Ministry of Labour and operates in full compliance with Georgian legislation.

This means our work is not a random or unorganised process. The company operates within an official legal framework and manages its relationship with candidates in a documented, transparent and responsible manner.

sections.title: Specialisation in Israel
sections.text:
Our primary and priority direction is Israel.

Work Visa Georgia does not attempt to work superficially across many countries simultaneously. Our practical experience, partner network and processes are primarily tailored to the Israeli labour market.

This allows us to better understand employer requirements, candidate preparation standards, documentation specifics and the stages a candidate actually needs to go through.

sections.title: Real Experience
sections.text:
Since 2024, Work Visa Georgia has already assisted over 120 candidates in finding employment in Israel.

The majority of these candidates are employed in the construction sector, with some in industrial directions.

For us, this is not just a number. This represents real experience, real processes, real candidates and real responsibility — both to the employed individuals and to the employing companies.

sections.title: International Partners
sections.text:
Work Visa Georgia works with approximately 10 active partner companies in Israel.

Our partners operate in the construction, industrial and service sectors. Our task is to provide each employer with candidates who best match the specific project's needs.

sections.title: Full Support
sections.text:
Our support does not end with registration.

Work Visa Georgia helps the candidate with:
bullets: Communication and adaptation after arriving in Israel, where possible

sections.title: Candidate Accountability System
sections.text:
One of Work Visa Georgia's important advantages is its candidate accountability system.

The company uses legal and administrative mechanisms that help ensure candidates take the process seriously, do not abandon agreed commitments and approach both the employer and the agency responsibly.

This is particularly important in the international recruitment sector, where one of the main risks for employers is a candidate refusing to depart or arbitrarily leaving work.

sections.title: Georgian Coordinator Support in Israel
sections.text:
Work Visa Georgia has a Georgian coordinator in Israel who, where possible, assists both candidates and employers with communication and organisational matters.

This support is particularly important at the initial stage of the candidate's arrival, when adaptation to the new environment, work process and employer takes place.

sections.title: Why Do Candidates Recommend Us?
sections.text:
Many new candidates contact Work Visa Georgia on the basis of a recommendation from a friend, relative or already-employed worker.

For us, this is the highest form of trust.

When someone already employed in Israel sends a new candidate to us, it means they consider our process, communication and support to be reliable.

procedure.title: How to Go to Work in Israel?
procedure.subtitle: The employment process through Work Visa Georgia
procedure.intro: Work Visa Georgia helps candidates through every stage of the legal employment process in Israel — from initial registration to departure.

duration.title: How long does the process take?
duration.text: In most cases, the process from registration to departure takes approximately 60 days on average. However, specific timelines may vary depending on the programme, profession, and employer requirements.

procedure.ctaTitle: Ready to start the employment process?
procedure.ctaText: Fill in the registration form and our team will contact you for an initial consultation.

steps.title: Registration
steps.desc: At the first stage, the candidate fills in an online application or has an initial consultation with a member of our team.

steps.title: Document Preparation
steps.desc: According to the relevant programme, the candidate prepares the required documents. Work Visa Georgia provides full administrative support throughout the documentation process.

steps.title: Presenting the Candidate to the Employer
steps.desc: After receiving and verifying documents, the candidate's profile is sent to the relevant employer.

steps.title: Employer Decision
steps.desc: If the employer approves the candidate, the next stage begins.
steps.note: Presenting a candidate does not mean automatic employment. The final decision is made by the employer and the relevant state authorities.

steps.title: Work Permit and Related Procedures
steps.desc: For approved candidates, the relevant administrative and legal procedures begin.

steps.title: Pre-Departure Preparation
steps.desc: Before departure, the candidate receives detailed information on all necessary matters.

steps.title: Arrival and Start of Employment
steps.desc: Upon arriving in Israel, the candidate reports to the employer or their representative and begins work according to the terms of the relevant contract.

steps.title: Post-Departure Support
steps.desc: Work Visa Georgia continues communicating with candidates and, where possible, assists them in resolving administrative and organisational matters.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions

items.q: How can I register?
items.a: Fill in our online form on the website or contact us via WhatsApp. Our team will reach you within 24 hours.

items.q: What documents do I need?
items.a: Basic: passport, ID, CV, medical certificate, criminal record. Full list on the documents page.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a: On average 4–8 weeks from application to departure, depending on documents and vacancies.

items.q: Is work experience required?
items.a: Experience is preferred for many vacancies, but not always required. We have vacancies for beginners too.

items.q: Is a passport required?
items.a: Yes, an international passport is required. Minimum 12 months validity recommended.

items.q: Is English or Hebrew required?
items.a: Not required. We help with translation and communication.

items.q: What happens after registration?
items.a: Our team contacts you for consultation, evaluates your profile, and offers suitable vacancies.

items.q: When will I know if I'm approved?
items.a: Employer decision usually comes within 1–2 weeks after the interview.

items.q: Can I apply for Israel if I worked there before?
items.a: Yes, if you had no legal issues during your previous visit. Tell us all details during consultation.

items.q: How do I contact WVG?
items.a: WhatsApp, phone, email or contact form. We work Mon–Fri 9:00–18:00.

items.q: Is a valid passport required?
items.a:
A valid passport is mandatory before departure.

Registration can begin in parallel with the process of renewing or obtaining a passport.

If the candidate is positively reviewed by the employer, they can obtain or renew their passport during the ongoing process.

items.q: What documents will I need?
items.a:
The list of documents depends on the programme, but typically includes:

Passport, ID card, criminal record certificate, medical certificate, work experience supporting documents, references, work video, and other additionally requested documents.

The full list of documents will be provided to the candidate based on the specific programme and employer requirements.

items.q: Why is a work video required?
items.a:
A work video is an important part of the candidate assessment process.

The video helps the employer to pre-assess the candidate's professional skills and real work experience.

For specialists, a work video is particularly important as it reduces the risk of inaccurate information or overstated experience.

items.q: Is having health problems an issue?
items.a:
Yes. Certain health problems may not meet programme requirements.

A candidate's health condition may become grounds for exclusion from the process if it prevents the performance of work or does not meet the specific programme requirements.

Each case is considered individually.

items.q: Can I participate if I have a criminal record?
items.a:
No. The existence of a criminal record is grounds for exclusion.

The candidate must present the relevant certificate if required within the specific programme.

items.q: Is military service required?
items.a:
No. Completion of military service is not a requirement.

This is not a mandatory condition for participation in the employment process in Israel.

items.q: Do tattoos or piercings cause problems?
items.a:
No. Tattoos or piercings do not affect the employment process.

What matters is the candidate's professional suitability, health condition, documentation and compliance with the employer's requirements.

items.q: Is it necessary to have previously worked in Israel?
items.a:
No. A lack of work experience in Israel is not an obstacle.

Candidates are assessed based on their professional experience, skills and the specific vacancy requirements.

items.q: What if I previously had undesirable guest status in Israel?
items.a:
Such candidates do not participate in the programme.

If a candidate has a legal or immigration status that prevents legal employment in Israel, their application cannot be reviewed.

items.q: I am already in Israel. Can you help?
items.a:
No. Work Visa Georgia only works on programmes for departure from Georgia to Israel.

The company does not handle legalisation or local employment matters for people already in Israel.

items.q: Can I register on a friend or relative's recommendation?
items.a:
Yes. Many candidates contact Work Visa Georgia on the basis of a recommendation.

If your friend, relative or acquaintance has already worked with our company, you can contact us with their recommendation.

items.q: Can I register from abroad?
items.a:
Yes. Registration is possible from both Georgia and abroad.

However, the process is ultimately linked to the programme of departure from Georgia to Israel and the preparation of required documents.

items.q: Can I apply for several vacancies at once?
items.a:
Yes. Candidates can express interest in several vacancies.

Ultimately, our team and the employer will assess which position best suits the candidate's experience and skills.

items.q: Can I also be registered with another agency?
items.a:
No. Active registration with another agency during the same period is not permitted.

This is necessary for the integrity of the process, correct communication with the employer and to avoid duplication of candidate data.

items.q: The employer declined. Is the process over?
items.a:
No. An employer's rejection does not automatically mean the process is over.

If the candidate's profile matches another current requirement, they may be presented to another employer.

items.q: Who makes the final decision?
items.a:
The final decision is always made by the foreign employer and the relevant state authorities.

Work Visa Georgia ensures the candidate's preparation, presentation and administrative coordination, but does not make the final employment decision.

items.q: Does Work Visa Georgia guarantee employment?
items.a:
No. Work Visa Georgia does not guarantee employment, a visa, a work permit or departure on a specific date.

The company ensures the recruitment, administrative support and coordination process, but final decisions depend on the employer and relevant state authorities.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a:
In most cases, the process from registration to departure takes approximately 60 days on average.

However, timelines may vary depending on the programme, employer requirements, document readiness and state procedures.

items.q: What happens after approval?
items.a:
After approval, the relevant administrative and legal procedures begin.

This may include the preparation of additional documents, final confirmation from the employer, state authority procedures and departure organisation.

items.q: Will you help me after departure?
items.a:
Yes. Work Visa Georgia continues communicating with candidates and, where possible, assists them in resolving administrative and organisational matters.

A local coordinator in Israel assists both candidates and employers with communication and adaptation matters.

items.q: Are the financial terms publicly available?
items.a:
No. The terms of service and financial matters are not published publicly.

They are determined on the basis of the specific programme, country, employer requirements and the contract concluded with the candidate.

Detailed information is provided to the candidate at the individual consultation and contract stage.

intro.title: Work Visa Georgia
intro.text1: Work Visa Georgia is an international employment agency focused on the legal employment of Georgian citizens in Israel.
intro.text2: Our activities include candidate selection, professional experience assessment, documentation organisation, employer presentation and pre-departure support.
intro.text3: Our main advantage is that we manage the process systematically — the candidate receives clear information from the outset, while the employer receives a pre-verified and prepared candidate.
